title: Avoid Nesting Try-Catch Blocks
description: Nesting try-catch blocks makes the source code difficult to read and understand as it is unclear which block will catch which exception. It is recommended to avoid this practice to maintain code quality.
recommendation: Refactor the code to avoid nesting try-catch blocks. Each block should be standalone and handle specific exceptions separately for clarity.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability by making exception handling clearer.

checks.message: Refactor the code to avoid nesting try-catch blocks. Each block should be standalone and handle specific exceptions separately for clarity.
